web.markdown:
### [armadillo](https://www.dictionary.com/browse/armadillo)

\[ahr-m _uh_- **dil**-oh\]


Get the Word of the Day

## Featured Articles

Read on to find out more about the bon mots that will add a French flair to your writing and conversations.](https://www.thesaurus.com/articles/16-french-loanwords-that-add-some-panache-to-english)

Learn about breaking up…with your partner's bed.](https://www.dictionary.com/culture/pop-culture/sleep-divorce) [**yassification  💅✨** \\
When you glam something up...way up!](https://www.dictionary.com/culture/memes/yassification) [**c'est la vie  🤷‍♀️🌍** \\
When life happens, this French saying fits.](https://www.dictionary.com/culture/translations/cest-la-vie) [**brat  💁‍♀️🟩** \\
Once just a kid term, it now has a cultural twist.](https://www.dictionary.com/culture/pop-culture/brat)

## Browse the Thesaurus

web.title: OneLook Thesaurus and Reverse Dictionary
web.description: You can use OneLook to find definitions, related words, quotes, names, lyrics, colors, and more. We've also got several unique and distraction-free word games.
web.markdown:
Play our new word game **[Cadgy](https://www.onelook.com/cadgy/?loc=tts)**! [OneLookThesaurus](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus?loc=lhp) **Thesaurus** Definitions Most similar A → Z Most modern Oldest Most formal (legal) Most funny-sounding Most lyrical Shortest Longest Most common Least common Z → A Most similar ...of top 20 ...of top 50 ...of top 100 ...of top 200 ...of all ...of top 100 Advanced filters Enter a word, phrase, description, or pattern above to find synonyms, related words, and more. See Help or try one of these examples: [joyful](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?loc=revfp&s=joyful) [honest](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?loc=revfp&s=honest) [ringing in the ears](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?loc=revfp&s=ringing+in+the+ears) [wet sound](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?loc=revfp&s=wet+sound) [spirit looking after](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?loc=revfp&s=spirit+looking+after) [a unwelcoming environment to live in](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?loc=revfp&s=a+unwelcoming+environment+to+live+in) **?** any letter**\*** any number of letters**#** consonant **@** vowel **-abcd** disallow letters**+abcd** restrict to letters**//abcd//** unscramble pattern **:** meaning #### How do I use OneLook's thesaurus / reverse dictionary? OneLook helps you find words for any type of writing. Similar to a traditional thesaurus, it find synonyms and antonyms, but it offers much greater depth and flexibility. Simply enter a single word, a few words, or even a whole sentence to describe what you need. Press Enter or choose from the autocomplete suggestions to explore related words. You can sort, filter, and explore the words that come back in a variety of creative ways. [Here's a video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AgPwm4FstXw) which goes over some of the basics. #### What are some examples? | | | | --- | --- | | | | | | --- | --- | | **Task** | **Example searches** | | 🔆 Find a word by describing it | [barrel maker](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?loc=revfp&s=barrel%20maker)
[before the flood](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?loc=revfp&s=before%20the%20flood)
[museum guide](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?loc=revfp&s=museum%20guide)
[search for food](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?loc=revfp&s=search%20for%20food)
[urge to travel](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?loc=revfp&s=urge%20to%20travel)
[what a milliner makes](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?loc=revfp&s=what+a+milliner+makes)
[tried twice for the same crime](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?loc=revfp&s=tried%20twice%20for%20the%20same%20crime)
[when cancer spreads through the body](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?loc=revfp&s=when%20cancer%20spreads%20through%20the%20body) | | 🔆 Explore synonyms and related concepts | [fancy](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?s=fancy&loc=revfp)
[baseball](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?s=baseball&loc=revfp)
[clouds](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?s=clouds&loc=revfp)
[twisty](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?s=twisty&loc=revfp)
[push](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?s=push&loc=revfp)
[industrial revolution](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?s=industrial+revolution&loc=revfp) | | 🔆 Get a list of words in some category ("type of...") | [type of bird of prey](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?s=type%20of%20bird%20of%20prey&loc=revfp)
[type of soft cheese](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?s=type%20of%20soft%20cheese&loc=revfp)
[type of light bulb](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?s=type%20of%20light%20bulb) | | 🔆 Find more words similar to some examples (comma-separated list) | [squishy,spongy,gooey](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?s=squishy%2C%20spongy%2C%20gooey)
[bicycle,motorcycle,scooter](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?s=bicycle%2C%20motorcycle%2C%20scooter)
[ice cream,pie,cookies](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?s=ice%20cream%2C%20pie%2C%20cookies) | | 🔆 Answer basic identification questions | [capital of Vietnam](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?s=capital%20of%20vietnam&loc=revfp)
[longest river in the world](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?s=longest%20river%20in%20the%20world)
[original host of Jeopardy](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?s=original+host+of+Jeopardy) | | 🔆 Solve crossword puzzle clues, or find words if you only know some of the letters.
(Use `[pattern](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/#patterns):description` syntax) | [??lon:synthetic fabric](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?loc=revfp&s=%3F%3Flon%3Asynthetic%20fabric)
[s?nt?:christmas](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?loc=revfp&s=s%3Fnt%3F%3Achristmas)
[l\*ch\*:fruit](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?loc=revfp&s=l*ch*%3Afruit)
[??????:hit](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?loc=revfp&s=%3F%3F%3F%3F%3F%3F%3Ahit)
[c\*:board game](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?loc=revfp&s=c*%3Aboard%20game) | | #### Exploring the results Click on any result to see definitions and usage examples tailored to your search, as well as links to follow-up searches and additional usage information when available. OneLook knows about more than 2 million different words and expressions covering every topic under the sun. Try exploring a favorite topic for a while and you'll be surprised how much new stuff there is to learn! #### Ordering the results Your results will initially appear with the most closely related word shown first, the second-most closely shown second, and so on. You can re-order the results in a variety of different ways, including alphabetically, by length, by popularity, by modernness, by formality, and by other aspects of style. Click the box that says "Closest meaning first..." to see them all. (Here's a short [video about sorting and filtering\\ with OneLook Thesaurus](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BnW17s3GxWs).) #### Filtering the results You can refine your search by clicking on the "Advanced filters" button on the results page. This lets you narrow down your results to match a certain starting letter, number of letters, number of syllables, related concept, meter, vowel sound, or number of syllables. Read [more details on filters](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/whatsnew/#filters) if you're interested in how they work. #### I'm only looking for synonyms! What's with all of these weird results? For some kinds of searches only the first result or the first few results are truly synonyms or good substitutions for your search word. We highlight these results in yellow. Beyond that, the results are meant to inspire you to consider similar words and adjacent concepts. Not all of the results will make sense at first, but they're all connected with your search in some way. We'd rather give you too many options than too few. If you're unsure of a word, we urge you to click on it to check its definitions and usage examples before using it in your Oscars acceptance speech or honors thesis. #### What are letter _patterns_? If you know some letters in the word you're looking for, you can enter a _pattern_ instead of, or in addition to, a description. Here are how patterns work: - The asterisk ( **`*`**) matches _any number of letters_. That means that you can use it as a placeholder for any part of a word or phrase. For example, if you enter `[blueb\*](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus?s=blueb*)` you'll get all the terms that start with "blueb"; if you enter `[\*bird](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus?s=*bird)` you'll get all the terms that end with "bird"; if you enter `[\*lueb\*](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?s=*lueb*)` you'll get all the terms that _contain_ the sequence "lueb", and so forth. An asterisk can match zero letters, too. - The question mark ( **`?`**) matches _exactly one letter_. That means that you can use it as a placeholder for a single letter or symbol. The query `[l?b?n?n](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?s=l%3Fb%3Fn%3Fn&ls=a)`, for example, will find the word "Lebanon". - The number-sign ( **`#`**) matches _any English consonant_. For example, the query `[tra#t](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?s=tra%23t&ls=a)` finds the word "tract" but not "trait". - The at-sign ( **`@`**) matches _any English vowel_ (including "y"). For example, the query `[abo@t](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?s=abo%40t&ls=a)` finds the word "about" but not "abort". - **NEW!** The comma ( **`,`**) lets you combine multiple patterns into one. For example, the query `[?????,\*y\*](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?s=%3F%3F%3F%3F%3F%2C*y*)` finds 5-letter words that contain a "y" somewhere, such as "happy" and "rhyme". - **NEW!** Use double-slashes ( **`//`**) before a group of letters to unscramble them (that is, find _anagrams_.) For example, the query `[//soulbeat](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?s=%2F%2Fsoulbeat)` will find "absolute" and "bales out", and `[re//teeprsn](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?s=re%2F%2Fteeprsn)` will find "represent" and "repenters". You can use another double-slash to end the group and put letters you're sure of to the right of it. For example, the query `[//blabrcs//e](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?s=%2F%2Fblabrcs%2F%2Fe)` will find "scrabble". Question marks can signify unknown letters as usual; for example, `[//we???](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?s=%2F%2Fwe???)` returns 5-letter words that contain a W and an E, such as "water" and "awake". - **NEW!** A minus sign ( **`-`**) followed by some letters at the end of a pattern means "exclude these letters". For example, the query `[sp???-ei](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?s=sp%3F%3F%3F-ei)` finds 5-letter words that start with "sp" but do not contain an "e"or an "i", such as "spoon" and "spray". - **NEW!** A plus sign ( **`+`**) followed by some letters at the end of a pattern means "restrict to these letters". For example, the query `[\*+ban](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?s=*%2Bban)` finds "banana". - On OneLook's main search or directly on OneLook Thesaurus, you can combine patterns and thesaurus lookups by putting a colon ( `:`) after a pattern and then typing a description of the word, as in `[??lon:synthetic fabric](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?loc=revfp&s=%3F%3Flon%3Asynthetic%20fabric)` and the other examples above. #### Other ways to access this service: - Drag this link to your browser's bookmarks bar for a convenient button that goes to the thesaurus: OneLook - Enter **`onelook.com/word`** into your browser's address bar to go directly to the OneLook Thesaurus entry for _word_. - We offer a [OneLook Thesaurus iPhone/iPad app](https://apps.apple.com/app/apple-store/id1615071061?pt=956323&ct=OLT%20help%20page%20link&mt=8) for a low subscription fee, with a two-week free trial. This gives you OneLook at your fingertips, and several cool app-only features, while helping us maintain the service for all! - If you use Google Docs, the thesaurus is integrated into the free [OneLook Thesaurus Google Docs Add-On](https://workspace.google.com/marketplace/app/onelook_thesaurus/372652075936) as the "Synonyms" button. (Wildcard patterns are not yet suppoerted by this add-on.) - If you regularly use the main OneLook site, you can put colon ( `:`) into any OneLook search box, followed by a description, to go directly to the thesaurus. - If you're a developer, the [Datamuse API](https://www.datamuse.com/api) gives you access to the core features of this site. #### Is this available in any language other than English? The same interface is now available in Spanish at [OneLook Tesauro](https://www.onelook.com/tesauro/) as a beta version. More languages are coming! #### How does it work? We use a souped-up version of our own [Datamuse API](https://datamuse.com/api/), which in turn uses several lingustic resources described in the "Data sources" section on that page. The definitions come from [Wiktionary](https://www.wiktionary.org/), [Wikipedia](https://www.wikipedia.org/), and [WordNet](https://wordnet.princeton.edu/). Here are some [known problems](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/whatsnew/#knownbugs) with the current system. Much gratitude to [Gultchin et al](https://arxiv.org/abs/1902.02783) for the algorithm behind the "Most funny-sounding" sort order. #### Profanity and problematic word associations If you're using this site with children, be forewarned you'll occasionally find profanity and other vulgar expressions. (The site aims to show you how words have been used in the language, including good ones and bad ones. Removing hurtful words would be a disservice to everyone!) Some of the thesaurus results come from a statistical analysis of the words in a large collection of books written in the past two centuries. A handful of times we've found that this analysis can lead us to suggest word associations that reflect racist or harmful stereotypes present in this source material. If you see one of these, please know that we do not endorse what the word association might imply. In egregious cases we will remove it from the site if you report it to us via the feedback link below. #### Privacy No personally identifying information is ever collected on this site or by any add-ons or apps associated with OneLook. OneLook Thesaurus sends your search query securely to the Datamuse API, which keeps a log file of the queries made to the service in the last 24 hours. The log file is deleted after 24 hours and we do not retain any long-term information about your IP address or invididual queries. #### Who's behind this site and where can I send my [~~comments and complaints~~](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/?loc=revfp&s=comments%20and%20complaints) feedback? OneLook is a service of [Datamuse](https://www.datamuse.com/). You can send us feedback [here](https://onelook.com/about.shtml?sel=olthes#feedback). The sunburst logo (🔆) is the emoji symbol for "high brightness", which we aspire to create with OneLook. (The graphic came from the open-source [Twemoji](https://github.com/twitter/twemoji) project.) [Home](https://www.onelook.com/) [Reverse Dictionary / Thesaurus](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/) [Saved words](https://www.onelook.com/saved) [Random word](https://www.onelook.com/random) [Subject index](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/subjects) [Word games](https://www.onelook.com/games) [Español](https://rimar.io/?adv_rel=1) [Spruce](https://www.onelook.com/spruce) [Feedback](https://onelook.com/about.shtml#feedback) [Privacy](https://onelook.com/about.shtml#privacy) [Dark mode](https://www.onelook.com/thesaurus/) Help You can use OneLook to find [definitions](https://www.onelook.com/random), [related words](https://www.onelook.com/random?related=1), [quotes](https://www.onelook.com/random?mentions=1), [names](https://www.onelook.com/random?names=1), [lyrics](https://www.onelook.com/random?verses=1), [colors](https://www.onelook.com/random?colors=1), and more. We've also got several unique and distraction-free [word games](https://www.onelook.com/games). Have you played [**Cadgy**](https://www.onelook.com/cadgy/?loc=cft) yet?

metadata.title: OneLook Thesaurus and Reverse Dictionary
metadata.keywords: thesaurus synonyms meanings reverse dictionary word finder search for words antonyms related similar words
metadata.og:description: A powerful English thesaurus and brainstorming tool that lets you describe what you're looking for in plain terms.  It gives you hundreds of related words to choose from and lets you narrow down the list with several advanced features.

web.title: Thesaurus - Synonyms, Antonyms, and Related Words
web.description: Free Thesaurus is a comprehensive online thesaurus of synonyms, antonyms, and related words. The thesaurus contains more than 145,800 unique entries from ...
web.markdown:
**Thesaurus - Synonyms, Antonyms, and Related Words** https://www.freethesaurus.com [Printer Friendly](https://www.freethesaurus.com/_/hp/Controls/printer-friendly.aspx?392) * * * | | # Thesaurus - Synonyms, Antonyms, and Related Words | | --- | | | Free Thesaurus is a comprehensive online thesaurus of synonyms, antonyms, and related words. The thesaurus contains more than 145,800 unique entries from three top sources: _Collins Thesaurus of the English Language - Complete and Unabridged_, _The American Heritage Roget's Thesaurus_, and _WordNet_.
- Search words or phrases to find synonyms, antonyms, and related words, all distinguished by color and grouped by meaning.
- Filter by part of speech to show only the results you want to see.
- Quickly find the synonyms for hundreds of thousands of words with Free Thesaurus. | | | ## Word Trivia | - [Share on Facebook](http://www.facebook.com/share.php?u=https://www.freethesaurus.com%2F_%2Farchive.htm.aspx%3Fshr%3Dt20560 "Share on Facebook")
- [Share on Twitter](https://twitter.com/share?url=https://www.freethesaurus.com%2F_%2Farchive.htm.aspx%3Fshr%3Dt20560&text=Word%20Trivia%3A%20sphere "Share on Twitter")
- [RSS feed](https://www.freethesaurus.com/_/WoD/rss.aspx?type=trivia "RSS feed")
- [E-mail](mailto:?subject=Word%20Trivia%3A%20sphere&body=Word%20Trivia%3A%20sphere%0d%0a%0d%0aURL%3a%20https://www.freethesaurus.com%2F_%2Farchive.htm.aspx%3Fshr%3Dt20560 "E-mail")
[](https://www.freethesaurus.com/ "Click to get this widget's code for your website or blog") | | --- | --- | | | Copy the code below and paste it where you want this content to be shown on your page or [**customize**](https://www.thefreedictionary.com/lookup.htm#Word-Trivia): | | ### Today's topic: [sphere](https://www.freethesaurus.com/sphere)
[**great circle**](https://www.freethesaurus.com/great+circle) \- Any circle on the surface of a sphere that lies on a plane through its center, or a circle that divides into two equal parts—as the Equator. [More...](https://www.thefreedictionary.com/great+circle)
[**quadrant**](https://www.freethesaurus.com/quadrant) \- A quarter of a circle or sphere. [More...](https://www.thefreedictionary.com/quadrant)
[**rhumb line**](https://www.freethesaurus.com/rhumb+line) \- A line on a sphere that cuts all meridians at the same angle; the path taken by a ship or plane that maintains a constant compass direction. [More...](https://www.thefreedictionary.com/rhumb+line)
[**diameter**](https://www.freethesaurus.com/diameter) \- From Greek, meaning "measure through" (a circle or sphere, etc.). [More...](https://www.thefreedictionary.com/diameter) | | | ## Mismatch | [](https://www.freethesaurus.com/ "Click to get this widget's code for your website or blog") | | --- | --- | | | Copy the code below and paste it where you want this content to be shown on your page or [**customize**](https://www.thefreedictionary.com/lookup.htm#Mismatch): | | | | | | --- | --- | | Select word: | Select opposite: | | host | incapableness | | unrestraint | parasite | | tightness | rejection | | capability | looseness | | acceptance | control | Match each word in the left column with its antonym (opposite) on the right. When finished, click Answer to see the results. Good luck! | | | ## Live Searches | | --- | | | Here's what people are searching for right now in Thesaurus:
[ski\|](https://www.freethesaurus.com/skin+colour) [price-to-earn\|](https://www.freethesaurus.com/price-to-earnings+ratio) [armoury](https://www.freethesaurus.com/armoury) [red cents](https://www.freethesaurus.com/red+cents) [tottered](https://www.freethesaurus.com/tottered) [gingerliness](https://www.freethesaurus.com/gingerliness) [Faustus](https://www.freethesaurus.com/Faustus) [republic](https://www.freethesaurus.com/republic) [ovenware](https://www.freethesaurus.com/ovenware) [arteria iliaca communis](https://www.freethesaurus.com/arteria+iliaca%20communis) [eosinopenia](https://www.freethesaurus.com/eosinopenia) [cystoid macular oedema](https://www.freethesaurus.com/cystoid+macular%20oedema) [Baldcypress](https://www.freethesaurus.com/Baldcypress) [root](https://www.freethesaurus.com/root) [Bayle's disease](https://www.freethesaurus.com/Bayle's+disease) [yield up](https://www.freethesaurus.com/yield+up) [charcoal-grey](https://www.freethesaurus.com/charcoal-grey) [Schick test](https://www.freethesaurus.com/Schick+test) [beta blocker eyedrop](https://www.freethesaurus.com/beta+blocker%20eyedrop) [inculpate](https://www.freethesaurus.com/inculpate) | ## Most popular searches - metadata.title: Thesaurus - Synonyms, Antonyms, and Related Words
metadata.description: Free Thesaurus: synonyms, antonyms, and related words. The thesaurus contains more than 145,800 unique entries from three top sources: Collins Thesaurus of the English Language - Complete and Unabridged, The American Heritage Roget's Thesaurus, and WordNet. Search words or phrases to find synonyms, antonyms, and related words, all distinguished by color and grouped by meaning. Filter by part of speech to show only the results you want to see. Quickly find the synonyms for hundreds of thousands of words with Free Thesaurus.
